page: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a custom 64-bit ARM architecture utilizing 32 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 7500 GB local NVMe SSD storage array and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory and database performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth, recording 406.47 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 183.77 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Passmark database operations and memory scores also rank in the top tier. Multi-core processing is robust, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21629.0 placing in the top tier, and Redis SET operations reaching 67.73 million operations per second. However, the architecture shows limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks, with prompt processing for a 70B parameter LLM model falling into the bottom 25% at 5.91 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i8g.8xlarge offers high resource density by combining a high-capacity local NVMe SSD with a power-efficient ARM-based processor architecture. This profile delivers high database and memory throughput per unit of compute resource, making it highly suitable for data-intensive workloads. Appropriate deployment scenarios include relational and non-relational databases, high-throughput Redis caching layers, software compilation, and static web serving. It is less suitable for heavy large language model inference or workloads requiring x86-specific instruction sets.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is an ARM-based, storage-optimized server instance utilizing 32 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, a 7500 GB local NVMe SSD, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth at 406.47 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 183.77 GB/sec, alongside top-tier Passmark database operation scores. While multi-core processing is strong, registering a Geekbench multi-core score of 21629.0, the instance exhibits weaker performance in large-scale LLM prompt processing. The high storage density and memory throughput make this instance highly efficient for database hosting, caching layers, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, a 7500 GB NVMe SSD, and top-tier memory and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge features 32 ARM64 cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 7500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSDs and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz.
7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
256.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth.
Top-tier performance in Passmark database operations and Redis SET transactions.
Suited for databases, caching layers, and high-throughput data processing.
